summary.en: The medieval heart of Edinburgh, characterized by towering tenement buildings, narrow alleys, and a dark, gothic atmosphere.
A stark contrast to the Old Town, this elegant grid of Georgian mansions, wide boulevards, and pristine gardens is the city's cultural and upmarket shopping district.
These neighborhoods offer a peaceful, bohemian, and village-like atmosphere, famous for their independent shopping scenes.
Edinburgh’s raffish, gritty, yet vibrant former port, known for its authentic traditional pubs and local charm.
Dominated by the University of Edinburgh, this area bursts with youthful energy and affordable places to eat and drink.
A busy, modern thoroughfare serving as the gateway to the city's sports culture, zoo, and contemporary art scene.

description.en: The city's premier interactive spot to learn about and taste Scotland's first national drink.
An excellent, independent arthouse cinema beloved by locals.
Highly recommended by locals for authentic, great-value, and cosmopolitan food.
A specific street noted for non-touristy, excellent value food near Greyfriars Bobby.
An amazing, free collection covering Scottish history, nature, and science that can easily absorb an afternoon.
Steep, winding streets known for their small, quirky independent stores rather than chain traps.
An excellent student-run theatre housed in an old church, home to The Improverts, Scotland's oldest improvised comedy troupe.
The massive extinct volcano looming directly over the city, offering panoramic views.
Look for bars pouring this specific local gin, which is distilled in and around the city.
This pedestrian-friendly lane is packed with traditional drinking spots, flanking George Street.
A local favorite for good value, independent food away from the overpriced main drags, running parallel to Princes Street.
Find a local fish and chip shop to try deep-fried pizza, black pudding, or haggis.
A miniature version of Arthur's Seat at the east end of Princes Street, featuring an ersatz Acropolis and incredibly accessible views.
A stunning, ornate red-sandstone building containing Scotland's history in portraits.
An upmarket, modern shopping enclave with designer labels next to St Andrew Square bus station.
Beautiful gardens filling the depression between Princes Street and the Old Town heights, with the railway tracks at their base.
Known for off-the-beaten-path pubs where locals go for a pint.
Look for this local gin, distilled nearby in North Berwick, in independent neighborhood bars.
Areas surrounding the local Tesco on Broughton Road host various independent spots for locals.
A fantastic street for excellent value, diverse food at the top of Leith Walk.
Extensive, beautiful gardens that offer a green retreat (note: historic glasshouses are closed until 2025).
A small, tranquil river that meanders right through the neighborhood, completely hiding the urban surroundings.
Famed for unique, non-chain retail, offering a distinct shopping experience.
A vibrant area in Canonmills with various local shops and a lively atmosphere.
The area is celebrated for authentic traditional pubs hosting regular live folk music.
The long, bustling avenue connecting the city to the port, offering numerous drinking spots.
A strictly local neighborhood area near Hibernian Stadium, known for its authentic eateries.
A waterfront area near Leith Bridge, great for local food spots with historic docks in the background.
The permanent mooring place of the famous royal ship at Ocean Terminal.
Europe's largest theatre boasting 3,000 seats, located at the top of Leith Walk.
A major shopping hub right on the waterfront, offering views over the Firth of Forth.
The glittering estuary merging into the North Sea, where cruise ships anchor and passengers are tendered ashore.
A major local beer brewery; hunt for their beers on tap in this area.
A beloved, historic local cinema in the Southside.
An area specifically noted by locals for excellent value food that heavily caters to diverse diets (vegan, halal, gluten-free).
Another prime strip for good, affordable meals, bustling with university students.
A massive green park that hosts the Meadows Festival and countless student gatherings.
An extensive, historic medieval ruin located further south from the city center.
A beautiful nature walk featuring a prominent telecom mast and panoramic views of the Edinburgh skyline.
The central hub of the university, contributing to the area's youthful and academic atmosphere.
One of the city's major local breweries; find a pub near Haymarket serving their guest ale.
An interesting arthouse cinema central to the Edinburgh International Film Festival.
A stretch running down to Haymarket, known for great value local food.
A massive cluster of local pubs and eating places surrounds this newly rebuilt railway hub.
The city's contemporary and modern art galleries, located west of Haymarket.
The massive home of Scottish Rugby Union, where the 6 Nations games are played.
The deep, wilder glen of the river, offering nature walks and cycling paths.
A newly rebuilt railway hub and a significant transport interchange in the west of the city.
An award-winning building in the Grassmarket area established to provide dance classes, workshops, and performances for the community.
A seductive little Italian restaurant in New Town that aims to provide a memorable and enjoyable dining experience.
A stylish restaurant that offers an affordable British-European set lunch in a chic atmosphere.
A mega-hyped Stockbridge bakery that is considered well worth the wait for its delicious baked goods.
A peaceful and beautiful park offering fantastic views over the Firth of Forth, perfect for relaxing in the sea air.
A small beach on the Firth of Forth that is a favorite local spot for wild swimming in the city.
A park holding the hidden treasure of 16th-century siege works, believed to be the only ones of their kind found in Britain.
An architectural gem featuring a beautiful swimming pool surrounded by an ornate, Baroque, cast-iron colonnade.
A cute and quaint hidden gem, this historic harbour is perfect for a walk and enjoying fish and chips by the sea.
A family-run Punjabi restaurant on Ferry Road, considered one of Edinburgh's must-visit culinary destinations.
A cool, all-day modern European-ish restaurant and wine bar showcasing Scottish produce that will make you want to become a regular.
A fabulous gourmet sandwich eatery in Marchmont known for its astonishingly delicious soups and homemade crumbles.
A cute, ferment-obsessed, country-style cafe in Marchmont perfect for sharing a few plates or grabbing a toastie to go.
One of Edinburgh’s most-loved restaurants in the West End, featuring an in-house bakery and moreish European cooking.

prompt.en: Find and photograph a bottle of whisky from your birth year.
Capture a photo of a whisky tasting glass, highlighting its color and clarity.
Take an artistic shot of their indie film marquee.
Photograph the classic entrance of the Cameo cinema, capturing its historic charm.
Capture the vibrant colors of the spices or a steaming plate of local cuisine.
Find and photograph an interesting architectural detail of the Central Mosque.
Find a restaurant sign or menu featuring a local Scottish legend (like a dog).
Photograph the bustling street scene of Forrest Road, focusing on a food stall or cafe exterior.
Find the oldest, strangest medieval artifact on display.
Capture the impressive architecture of the museum's Grand Gallery or main hall.
Capture the perfect curve of Victoria Street's colorful storefronts from the terrace above.
Photograph a unique or quirky shopfront display on either Victoria or Cockburn Street.
Snap a selfie in front of the historic, imposing gothic exterior.
Find and photograph a poster for an upcoming student-run show at Bedlam Theatre.
Find the 'simple ascent' path on the far side and take a panoramic shot from the summit.
Capture a photo that highlights the rugged, volcanic landscape of Arthur's Seat.
Order a creative cocktail featuring Edinburgh Gin and photograph the garnish.
Find and photograph a bottle of Edinburgh Gin in a local bar or shop.
Find a pub offering a 'guest ale' and ask the bartender for a tasting sample.
Capture the lively atmosphere of Rose Street, focusing on a traditional pub exterior.
Photograph a beautifully written, chalk-board lunch special.
Take a photo of the charming facade of an independent eatery on Thistle Street.
Order your chips with the uniquely Edinburgh 'salt'n'sauce' (a runny, vinegary brown sauce) and photograph the distinct condiment.
Photograph a unique deep-fried item from a chippy, like a deep-fried Mars bar or haggis.
Frame the Old Town skyline through the stone pillars of the National Monument.
Capture an iconic shot of the circular Dugald Stewart Monument against the city backdrop.
Locate a portrait of a famous native Edinburgh author, like Sir Walter Scott or Sir Arthur Conan Doyle.
Photograph the stunning, ornate interior architecture of the Scottish National Portrait Gallery.
Capture an interesting architectural reflection in one of the luxury store windows.
Take a photo of a prominent designer storefront on Multrees Walk.
Find and photograph a vibrant floral display within Princes Street Gardens.
Capture a scenic shot of Edinburgh Castle towering above Princes Street Gardens.
Find a pub hosting a live, traditional Scottish folk music session.
Photograph the cozy and traditional interior of a Stockbridge pub.
Photograph the bartender pouring a local spirit, ideally NB Gin.
Find and photograph a creative display of NB Gin bottles in a local establishment.
Find a locally baked treat in an independent shop window.
Capture the charming exterior of a local independent shop in Canonmills.
Photograph a vibrant dish from a global cuisine spot on Elm Row.
Take a wide shot of Elm Row, showcasing its diverse range of eateries.
Find the most exotic plant thriving in the chilly Scottish climate.
Photograph a serene pathway winding through the Royal Botanic Garden.
Snap a serene photo of the river or local wildlife along the Water of Leith.
Capture a shot that shows how the Water of Leith hides the urban surroundings.
Find the quirkiest piece of local art or vintage clothing on display.
Photograph an interesting interior detail of one of Stockbridge's independent shops.
Capture the lively atmosphere of Broughton Road, focusing on local activity.
Find and photograph a unique sign from a local business on Broughton Road.
Record a short snippet or take a photo of an acoustic guitar or fiddle performance.
Photograph the exterior of a traditional Leith folk pub, capturing its character.
Take a photo of a pint of a local guest ale against a gritty, urban backdrop.
Capture the bustling street life and diverse architecture along Leith Walk.
Find a bakery or cafe subtly showing the green colors of the local 'Hibs' football team.
Photograph a sign from a local, non-touristy eatery in the Easter Road area.
Photograph your lunch with the historic docks in the background.
Capture a scenic view of the Commercial Quay waterfront, including boats or reflections.
Frame a selfie to capture the sheer scale of the Royal Yacht Britannia.
Photograph an interesting detail on the deck or exterior of the Royal Yacht Britannia.
Snap the massive grand marquee of a West End show at the Edinburgh Playhouse.
Capture the grand entrance of Europe's largest theatre, the Edinburgh Playhouse.
Find the best indoor vantage point looking out over the Firth of Forth.
Photograph a unique architectural feature or bustling scene inside Ocean Terminal.
Capture a photo of a cruise ship anchored in the Firth of Forth (if visible).
Photograph the wide expanse of the Firth of Forth, highlighting the natural beauty of the estuary.
Find a tap handle featuring the Stewart Brewing logo.
Photograph a glass of Stewart Brewing beer, showcasing its color and head.
Capture the vintage seating or classic ticket booth of the Dominion cinema.
Photograph the distinctive art deco facade of the Dominion cinema.
Find a menu offering a unique vegan twist on a traditional Scottish dish.
Capture a photo that highlights the diverse range of food options available in Tollcross.
Photograph a bustling, atmospheric dining room full of university students.
Find and photograph a menu board advertising an appealing and affordable meal.
Take an action shot of locals playing sports or having a barbecue in The Meadows.
Capture a photo that embodies the spirit of community and relaxation in the park.
Find the most hidden nook or cranny within the medieval stone walls.
Photograph the impressive scale and ruinous beauty of Craigmillar Castle.
A panoramic shot of the Edinburgh skyline from the hillside.
Capture an interesting perspective of the prominent telecom mast on Blackford Hill.
Photograph one of the historic buildings on the University of Edinburgh campus.
Capture a candid moment of student life on the university campus.
Find a pub near Haymarket serving a Caledonian guest ale.
Photograph the logo or branding of Caledonian Brewery on a bottle or tap.
Photograph the current indie film poster display at Filmhouse.
Capture a photo that evokes the arthouse cinema and film festival atmosphere of Filmhouse.
Find the most appetizing, hand-written lunchtime special board.
Photograph a selection of diverse food options available on Queensferry Street.
Capture a classic pub exterior contrasting with a modern tram gliding past.
Photograph an interesting interior detail of a traditional pub near Haymarket Station.
Recreate a pose from an abstract sculpture in the gallery grounds.
Photograph a piece of modern art that particularly catches your eye inside one of the galleries.
Capture the sheer scale of the Murrayfield Stadium exterior.
Find and photograph a symbol or sign representing Scottish Rugby at the stadium.
Spot a local cyclist or walker navigating the off-street dirt paths.
Photograph the wilder, natural scenery of the Water of Leith's upper glen.

description.en: A newly rebuilt railway hub and a significant transport interchange in the west of the city.

prompt.en: Capture a photo of the modern architecture of the rebuilt Haymarket Station.
Take an artistic, low-angle photo of a cobblestone street. Try to capture the texture and history under your feet.
Find a narrow alleyway or 'close' off the Royal Mile and take a picture looking from the darkness into the light of the main street.
Take a selfie with a building that looks like it has a thousand stories to tell. What's its story?
Spot a ghost tour group. Without being intrusive, capture the spooky atmosphere or an interesting character.
Find the most interesting or oldest-looking pub sign and take a photo of it.
Photograph the most colourful or unusual tartan pattern you can find, whether on a kilt, scarf, or souvenir.
Capture a photo of the Old Town's skyline, focusing on the silhouettes of its many church steeples and historic rooftops.
Find a shop window displaying an impressive collection of Scotch whisky and take a photo of the 'water of life'.
Capture a moment of a street performer's act on the Royal Mile. A piper, a magician, or a musician!
Find a bakery or cafe selling a traditional Scottish treat like a scone or shortbread. Take a delicious-looking photo before you eat it.
Find an interesting architectural detail on an old building – a gargoyle, a crest, or an old inscription – and take a close-up shot.
Capture a reflection of an old building in a puddle, a shop window, or even someone's sunglasses.
The Old Town is full of stairs. Find a particularly long or winding set of steps and take a photo looking up or down.
Find a hidden garden, a colourful window box, or a patch of moss growing on an ancient wall. Capture this touch of nature amidst the stone.
Take a photo that captures the energy and movement of the crowds exploring the historic streets.
Take a photo that captures the symmetry and elegance of a Georgian terrace or crescent. Look for repeating patterns in the windows and doors.
Find the most impressive-looking front door – think grand pillars, a shiny brass knocker, and a bold colour. Take a selfie in front of it.
Find a high-end shop on George Street or Multrees Walk and photograph its creative and luxurious window display.
Discover a quiet mews lane behind the grand main streets. Capture the charming, village-like atmosphere.
Take a close-up photo of the intricate iron railings and balconies that are a signature feature of New Town architecture.
Find a chic cafe and take a picture of your coffee or tea against an elegant backdrop.
Many squares have private gardens for residents. Find one and (from the outside!) take a photo peeking through the railings at the hidden greenery.
Capture the classic design of a New Town streetlight, either during the day or as it begins to glow at dusk.
Use one of the large, clean windows of a gallery or shop to capture a reflection of the street scene opposite.
Find one of the many statues in the New Town's squares. Take a photo from the statue's perspective, looking out at what it 'sees' every day.
Find a street that still has its original cobblestones and capture the contrast between the old stones and the grand, smooth buildings.
Look down! Take a photo of the basement-level entrances and windows that are a unique feature of New Town architecture.
Even in the bustling New Town, there are quiet moments. Find a peaceful side street or square and capture the tranquility.
The New Town has many stylish pubs. Find one and take a picture that captures the sophisticated pub atmosphere.
Look up and capture the uniform lines of the New Town rooftops and their chimney pots against the sky.
Capture a photo that shows the 'village within a city' feel of Stockbridge. This could be a quaint shopfront, a quiet street, or a local chatting.
Find the most interesting, quirky, or vintage item you can see in a charity shop window and take a photo of it.
Find a calm spot on the Water of Leith and capture the reflection of the surrounding trees and buildings on the water's surface.
Stockbridge is full of artisan food shops (bakers, cheesemongers, delis). Take a mouth-watering photo of a display of delicious local produce.
Find a particularly colourful or well-kept tenement building and take a photo that highlights its charm.
Take a selfie on one of the bridges crossing the Water of Leith, with the water and greenery in the background.
Photograph the storefront of an independent shop with a unique name or interesting design.
Stockbridge is very dog-friendly. Capture a photo of a cute dog enjoying a walk or waiting patiently outside a shop.
If it's a Sunday, capture the bustling atmosphere of the Stockbridge Market. If not, find a spot where the market is held and imagine the scene.
Find a traditional pub and take a photo that captures its cozy, welcoming atmosphere.
Many shops and homes have beautiful flower boxes or hanging baskets. Find the most vibrant floral display and snap a picture.
In Canonmills, capture a photo that shows the mix of old stone buildings and newer developments.
Find the St Stephen's Church clock tower and take a photo of it from an interesting angle.
Find an entrance to the Water of Leith walkway and take a photo looking down the path, inviting the viewer to explore.
Find a bench with a nice view and take a photo from the perspective of someone sitting down to relax with a book.
Find something that hints at Leith's maritime past – an anchor, a ship's name on a pub, a seagull, or a view of the water.
Leith is known for its street art. Find a cool mural or piece of graffiti and take a creative photo of it.
Capture the mix of old industrial buildings and modern apartments. Find a shot that shows the contrast between old and new Leith.
Leith is a melting pot of cultures. Take a photo of a restaurant, shop, or sign that represents a country far from Scotland.
Head to The Shore or the waterfront and take a selfie with the water and boats in the background.
Take a photo looking down the long, straight stretch of Leith Walk, capturing the buses, people, and the endless line of shops.
Find a pub that looks like it's been there for centuries. Take a photo of its classic exterior.
There are several bridges crossing the Water of Leith in this area. Frame a photo looking through or over one of them.
Many of the old tenement buildings have hidden courtyards or back greens. Find a view into one of these secret spaces.
Find a seafood restaurant and take a photo of its menu or a fresh display. Capture the essence of food from the sea.
Look up! Take a low-angle shot of the tall, imposing sandstone tenement buildings that define much of Leith.
At The Shore, capture the reflection of the colourful buildings in the water of the harbour.
Leith has a unique character. Find a shop or pub sign with a funny, strange, or very 'Leith' name and photograph it.
Find a park or a leafy green space like Leith Links or Pilrig Park and capture a moment of peace away from the busy streets.
Find a spot where you can see the open water of the Firth of Forth. Take a photo looking out to sea.
Take a photo that captures the scale and life of The Meadows. People relaxing, playing sports, or just the long, tree-lined paths.
Capture a scene that says 'university'. It could be students with books, a university building, or a cafe full of people studying.
Take a selfie in front of an impressive-looking University of Edinburgh building. Look thoughtful!
This area is full of affordable, delicious food. Find a place with a great lunch deal or a tempting menu and snap a photo of it.
Find a theatre, like the Festival Theatre or Bedlam Theatre, and take a photo of its entrance or a show poster.
Find a quiet courtyard or garden within the university campus and capture the peaceful, studious atmosphere.
The Marchmont area has beautiful, grand tenement buildings. Take a photo that shows off their distinctive architecture.
Take a photo that includes two key elements of student life: a bicycle and a backpack.
Photograph a street that has restaurants from at least three different continents on it.
Find a group of people enjoying The Meadows or Bruntsfield Links and capture the moment.
Find a spot where a historic university building stands next to a modern one. Capture the architectural contrast.
Take a photo of a cozy-looking coffee shop, a hub for students and locals alike.
Many streets in this area are lined with trees. Take a photo down a long, leafy residential street.
Look for small, interesting details on the buildings – a date stone, a small carving, or a unique piece of stained glass.
From The Meadows, you can often see Arthur's Seat or the Salisbury Crags. Frame a photo with the park in the foreground and the hills in the back.
Capture the constant motion around Haymarket Station – the trams, trains, buses, and people all coming and going.
The West End is famous for its elegant crescents. Find one and take a wide photo that shows its beautiful curve.
Find a piece of public art or sculpture, perhaps near the Modern Art galleries, and take a photo of it.
Find a pub decorated with rugby memorabilia or a sign pointing towards Murrayfield Stadium. Capture the sporting spirit of the area.
Take a selfie with the iconic clock of Haymarket Station in the background.
The West End has many hidden basement pubs and classic bars. Find one that looks inviting and photograph its entrance.
Take a photo from the perspective of someone waiting for a tram, looking down the tracks as it approaches.
If you're near the edge of the neighborhood, find a view looking towards the picturesque Dean Village and the Water of Leith.
This area mixes business with leisure. Capture a photo showing a modern office building next to a traditional pub or restaurant.
Find a restaurant with a particularly stylish or elegant exterior and take a photo.
Step off the main roads and find a quiet residential street. Capture the peaceful contrast to the nearby transport hub.
Look for interesting lines and shapes in the architecture, whether from the Victorian station or the Georgian townhouses.
Take a photo that captures the morning coffee rush at a cafe near the station.
On a sunny day, find interesting shadows cast by the grand buildings or the station's structure.
From certain streets in the West End, you can get a glimpse of Edinburgh Castle. Find a view and frame the castle in the distance.
